title: sida variegata
intro: Sida variegata, also known as the country mallow or Indian mallow, is a plant species native to tropical and subtropical regions of the world. It is a perennial herb with medicinal properties, traditionally used to treat various health issues due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant has been found to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving respiratory problems, and treating wounds. Sida variegata can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, making it a versatile and accessible remedy in traditional medicine.
